daily_candidate.summary: Single-case lesson: in nearby crypto touch markets, verification-surface caution should not receive the same discount as true event-nonoccurrence when touch-style settlement, sub-1% distance to threshold, and multi-day residual window already make a touch highly plausible.
daily_candidate.why: This is specific, operational, and behavior-changing, but still only supported by one reviewed case, so it fits daily memory rather than durable memory. It is not already stated in current durable memory, which only contains role-level rules. The review’s strongest reusable lesson is the need to separate lack of proof from evidence that the event likely has not happened yet in nearby touch setups.
